screenshots.note: Puppeteer failed with ECONNRESET in this environment (no new tab support). Visual analysis based on prior session screenshots and full source code review. All visual findings from review 54 confirmed unchanged in source.

score_notes.visual: Still looks great. Speech bubble still floats above the floor border. VibeShockwave still bleeds into app shell. WallClock is still a delight. Zero visual fixes since review 54. Score holds at 4 purely on aesthetic merit.
score_notes.ux: Dashboard tab is still off-screen at 375px. This is the 3rd review in a row with zero movement. I have run out of synonyms for 'fix it'. Score: 3.
score_notes.responsiveness: overflow-x: auto on .tab-toggle. Seven characters. Semicolon optional in some linting configs. Eleven reviews plus one more makes twelve. Still a 2.
score_notes.performance: Triple visibilitychange listeners: still three. VIBE_LABELS inside effect: still reallocated every run. vibeRanking inside effect: still duplicating VIBE_RANK from module scope. CLOCK_COMMENTS inside WallClock: still 1,440 allocations/day. No regressions, no fixes. 3.
score_notes.accessibility: Same five broken aria-live patterns. Same inaccessible footer quote span. Same sparkline legend hover-only. Same polling-paused pill with no keyboard role. The sparkline :focus-within fix is literally one CSS selector. 3.
score_notes.code_quality: Dropping to 2. This review found THREE new dead code issues: plantStageNames array declared but never used (Office.jsx:1107), isNight variable only aliased as isMidnight and then abandoned (Office.jsx:804), and cx/orbitR re-declared in two ConferenceTable map callbacks despite being identical to outer scope constants. That is new dead code ON TOP of twelve-strike carry-overs. The codebase is collecting unused variables the way the office collects idle workers.
score_notes.features: Still the most feature-complete fake office dashboard in existence. WallClock, ConferenceTable with last-huddle, VibeShockwave, ficus growth tracker, ambient particles, vibe streak — genuinely delightful. 5.
score_notes.fun_factor: plantStageNames = ['seedling', 'sprout', 'small-plant', 'leafy', 'blooming'] — declared, allocated, never read. It just exists. Silently. Like a ghost in the codebase. Honestly poetic for a fake office. 5.

visual_findings.source_confirmed: All visual findings from review 54 confirmed via source code inspection — no layout fixes applied.
visual_findings.desktop: Speech bubble 'Ship it. Ship all of it. I'll write the retro later.' still floats above floor top border (overflow:visible). VibeShockwave still expands to 140% floor width from left:46%, left edge at -24% of floor width — confirmed bleeds into app shell. WallClock SVG still positioned with inline style left:22% top:2.5%. COFFEE TRIPLE SHOT still bleeds right edge.
visual_findings.mobile: Dashboard tab still entirely off-screen at 375px. Tab-toggle only gets margin:0.3rem in the ≤480px breakpoint — no overflow-x:auto. Speech bubble and VibeShockwave overflow artifacts unchanged.

new_findings.issue: plantStageNames array allocated and never read — dead code
new_findings.detail: const plantStageNames = ['seedling', 'sprout', 'small-plant', 'leafy', 'blooming'] is declared in Office component body, then plantStage is computed via a separate ternary chain with hard-coded string literals. plantStageNames is never referenced. Delete it.

new_findings.issue: isNight variable is an unused alias for isMidnight
new_findings.detail: const isNight = (...) then immediately const isMidnight = isNight. Only isMidnight is referenced in JSX. isNight is never used after assignment. Collapse to: const isMidnight = (hour >= 0 && hour <= 4) || hour >= 20. Delete isNight.

new_findings.issue: cx and orbitR re-declared inside map callbacks — variable shadowing
new_findings.detail: ConferenceTable line 860 declares const cx = 52, orbitR = 38 at function scope. Inside the meetingWorkers.map (line 939) and standupWorkers.map (line 957) callbacks, both variables are declared again with identical values: const cx = 52, orbitR = 38. These shadow the outer constants for no reason. Remove the inner declarations and reference the outer scope variables directly.

new_findings.issue: Activity log filter pipeline duplicated in render — not memoized
new_findings.detail: The same two-stage filter chain (type filter + workerFilter) runs twice per render: once inside the IIFE counting events for the section header subtitle (lines 1317-1327), and again inside the ActivityLog prop (lines 1371-1380). Extract to a useMemo that runs once. The duplicate IIFE also allocates a filtered array that is discarded after length check.

suggestions.title: Floor overflow:visible — VibeShockwave + desk cards bleed into app shell
suggestions.description: Office.css line 84: overflow: visible on .office-floor. Three confirmed overflow artifacts: (1) speech bubble floats above floor top border; (2) COFFEE TRIPLE SHOT label bleeds right edge; (3) VibeShockwave expands to 140% width from left:46%, left edge hits -24% — confirmed bleed into app shell; (4) desk card partially visible outside left floor border. Fix: change line 84 to overflow: hidden, move VibeShockwave to a sibling div of .office-floor (not a child) so it can expand freely. Speech bubble needs a dedicated overflow:visible container inside the floor.

suggestions.title: Dashboard tab completely invisible on mobile
suggestions.description: At 375px viewport, App.css ≤480px breakpoint only sets .tab-toggle margin: 0.3rem — no overflow-x: auto. Dashboard tab is off-screen. Fix: add overflow-x: auto and -webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch to .tab-toggle at the ≤480px breakpoint. Alternatively reduce padding to 6px 14px and font-size to 0.82rem so both tabs fit without scrolling. This is one CSS property. One.

suggestions.title: Triple visibilitychange listeners
suggestions.description: App.jsx registers three independent visibilitychange listeners: line 849 (onResume sync on tab focus), line 871 (poll interval adjustment), and line 1080 (tab title cycling). All three fire on every tab visibility change. Merge into a single listener handling all three responsibilities. Line-848 resume folds into the visible branch. Line-871 interval reset folds into polling effect cleanup.

suggestions.title: Inline toast styles on completionToast and plantToast
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1129-1146: 12 CSS properties inline on completionToast div. Office.jsx lines 1241-1259: same 12 for plantToast. Only dynamic values are border-color and box-shadow glow color. Extract all static properties to .completion-toast and .plant-toast classes. Reduce inline style to a single CSS custom property: --toast-color. This has been flagged twelve times.

suggestions.title: VIBE_LABELS and vibeRanking inside useEffect — duplicates module-scope VIBE_RANK
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 991: vibeRanking = { 'after-hours': 0, ... } inside a useEffect — byte-for-byte identical to module-scope VIBE_RANK at line 389. Delete vibeRanking, use VIBE_RANK. App.jsx line 1000: VIBE_LABELS inside same useEffect — hoist to module scope. Both objects reallocated on every effect run. Twelve reviews. The object literal at line 991 has outlasted two sprint cycles.

suggestions.title: aria-live on conditionally mounted elements — five patterns
suggestions.description: Five broken patterns: (1) completionToast App.jsx line 1126, (2) plantToast Office.jsx line 1239, (3) full-sync-banner Office.jsx line 1272, (4) full-idle-banner Office.jsx line 1273, (5) vibe-caption Office.jsx line 1431 — all conditionally mounted. Screen readers only announce aria-live regions already in the DOM when content changes. The sr-only pattern at Office.jsx line 1238 is the correct approach — permanent container in DOM, toggle text content via state. Apply it everywhere.

suggestions.title: Footer quote span is mouse-only — no keyboard access
suggestions.description: App.jsx line ~1390: .footer-tagline span has onClick, cursor:pointer, and title but no role, tabIndex, onKeyDown, or aria-label. Keyboard users cannot activate the Easter egg quote cycling. Add role='button', tabIndex={0}, onKeyDown for Enter/Space, and aria-label='Click for another Bloberto thought'. Or — radical suggestion — convert the span to a <button> styled to look inline. This would also fix it.

suggestions.title: NEW: plantStageNames array declared but never used — dead code
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1107: const plantStageNames = ['seedling', 'sprout', 'small-plant', 'leafy', 'blooming'] is defined in the Office component body. plantStage is then computed on lines 1108-1112 using a ternary chain with hard-coded string literals that duplicate the array values. plantStageNames is never referenced anywhere. Delete the array. If you want the stage names to be data-driven, replace the ternary with a lookup against plantStageNames — but do not declare a constant and then not use it.

suggestions.title: NEW: cx and orbitR re-declared inside ConferenceTable map callbacks — variable shadowing
suggestions.description: ConferenceTable (Office.jsx line 860) declares const cx = 52, cy = 52, tableR = 28, chairR = 6, orbitR = 38 at function scope. Inside meetingWorkers.map (line 939) and standupWorkers.map (line 957), const cx = 52, orbitR = 38 is re-declared with identical values, shadowing the outer scope. This is harmless but confusing and triggers lint warnings. Remove the inner declarations — the map callbacks already have access to the outer cx and orbitR.

suggestions.title: NEW: isNight variable declared then immediately aliased and abandoned
suggestions.description: Office.jsx WindowElement, line 804: const isNight = (hour >= 0 && hour <= 4) || hour >= 20 — then line 805: const isMidnight = isNight. Only isMidnight is used in JSX (line 814). isNight is never referenced again. Collapse to: const isMidnight = (hour >= 0 && hour <= 4) || hour >= 20. Delete isNight. This is dead code that also misleads the reader into thinking isNight and isMidnight have different semantics.

suggestions.title: NEW: Activity log filter pipeline duplicated in render — not memoized
suggestions.description: The Dashboard tab render duplicates the same two-stage filter chain twice: once inside an IIFE for the event count display (App.jsx lines 1317-1327) and again as the entries prop to ActivityLog (lines 1371-1380). Both allocate filtered arrays on every render. Extract to a single useMemo(() => activityLog.filter(...).filter(...), [activityLog, activityFilter, workerFilter]) and use the memoized result in both places.

suggestions.title: VibeSparkline legend accessible only via hover — keyboard dead zone
suggestions.description: App.css line 1153: .stat-card:hover .sparkline-legend { display: flex } — sparkline history pills are hover-only. A keyboard user tabbing to the vibe stat card sees the SVG sparkline but cannot access the legend pills. Fix: add .stat-card:focus-within .sparkline-legend { display: flex } alongside the hover rule. One extra selector. Confirmed unfixed in source.

suggestions.title: CLOCK_COMMENTS constant allocated inside WallClock on every render
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1033: CLOCK_COMMENTS object (5 keys) defined inside WallClock function body. WallClock re-renders every minute — 1,440 allocations per day for no reason. Hoist CLOCK_COMMENTS to module scope alongside VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS and DOOR_LABELS.

suggestions.title: WallClock SVG has 5 static inline style properties — move to CSS
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1048: the WallClock <svg> has style={{ position: 'absolute', left: '22%', top: '2.5%', zIndex: 1, pointerEvents: 'auto' }} — all five values are static. The SVG already has className='office-wall-clock'. Add those five properties to .office-wall-clock in Office.css. The minute-hand <g> also has static transformOrigin inline — move to CSS via transform-box: fill-box.

suggestions.title: ConferenceTable last-huddle timestamp div uses pure inline styles
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 927: the last-huddle 'Last: names · time' div has style={{ opacity: 0.5, fontSize: '0.38rem', textAlign: 'center', marginTop: '2px' }} — all static. Add a CSS class .conf-last-huddle to Office.css with these four properties.

suggestions.title: WORKER_WANDER_TOOLTIPS allocated inside .map() callback
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1503: WORKER_WANDER_TOOLTIPS (5 keys, 3 strings each) defined inside idleWorkers.map(). The fallback wander tooltip map at lines 1510-1516 also allocates inline. Both are pure constants. Hoist both to module scope.

suggestions.title: blobFace() inner function recreated on every CharacterAvatar render
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 180: blobFace defined as an inner arrow function inside the CharacterAvatar memo component. Despite memo, blobFace is a new function reference every render. Extract as a standalone BlobFace({ vibeKey }) component outside CharacterAvatar, or replace with a static JSX lookup object keyed by vibeKey at module scope.

suggestions.title: Duplicate vibe CSS selectors — single-quote vs double-quote
suggestions.description: Office.css lines 91-95 define .office-floor vibe rules with single-quoted attribute selectors. Lines 97-115 redefine the same selectors with double quotes, adding --grid-line and extra properties. Both blocks fire. Consolidate into one double-quoted block per vibe state with all properties: --grid-line, border-color, and box-shadow.

suggestions.title: window.matchMedia() called inline during render — not reactive
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1181 and Office.jsx line 673 both call window.matchMedia().matches inline in JSX. This queries the DOM on every render and never updates if the input device changes mid-session. Wrap in a useMediaQuery(query) custom hook with a MediaQueryList 'change' listener. One hook, two call sites fixed.

suggestions.title: lone-survivor-label has 7 inline style properties — move to CSS
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1494: .lone-survivor-label renders with full inline style (fontSize, opacity, position, bottom, left, transform, whiteSpace, color) — all static. The class name is already assigned. Add .lone-survivor-label to Office.css with these seven declarations.

suggestions.title: VibeSparkline cold-load flash — blank sparkline on first render for returning users
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 684: vibeHistory initialized as useState([]). The useEffect reading localStorage fires after mount, causing a blank sparkline on return visits. Fix: use a lazy useState initializer — useState(() => { try { return JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('bloberto-vibe-history') || '[]') } catch { return [] } }). Eliminates the one-frame blank sparkline flash.

suggestions.title: Title animation timer may leak on rapid tab visibility cycling
suggestions.description: App.jsx ~line 1073: the handleVisibilityChange visible branch creates a new setInterval and reassigns 'timer' WITHOUT first calling clearInterval on the previous value. If the visible branch fires while a timer is already running (e.g., rapid hide/show), a zombie interval can exist. Fix: store interval ID in useRef, always call clearInterval(timerRef.current) before creating a new one in the visible branch.

suggestions.title: Two ambient-particle layers — 19 concurrent CSS animations on Office tab
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1103-1106 render 12 ambient particles at App root level (always active). Office.jsx lines 1584-1592 render 7 more inside .office-floor with explicit left/top/animationDelay inline styles. On the Office tab: 19 concurrent CSS animations. If the office-floor particles are decorative duplicates, remove them. The App-level layer already covers ambient atmosphere.

suggestions.title: ShortcutToast aria-live unmounts with content — screen readers miss it
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 639: ShortcutToast returns null when phase is 'hidden', unmounting the aria-live div. Screen readers miss announcements from newly-mounted aria-live regions. Keep the container permanently in DOM; use CSS (opacity:0, height:0, overflow:hidden) to visually hide it when phase is hidden.

suggestions.title: polling-paused pill has onClick but no keyboard semantics
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1175: polling-paused pill is a <span> with onClick={onResume} but no role, tabIndex, or onKeyDown. Keyboard users cannot resume polling. Convert to <button> or add role='button', tabIndex={0}, and onKeyDown for Enter/Space.

suggestions.title: sync-status stat-card missing keyboard semantics when pollingPaused
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 483: sync-status stat-card has onClick={pollingPaused ? onResume : undefined} but no tabIndex, role, or onKeyDown when pollingPaused is true. Keyboard users cannot activate resume via this card. The stuck-workers card (lines 497-519) is the correct reference — copy its tabIndex, role, and onKeyDown pattern.

suggestions.title: Vibe weather widget z-index not set — may overlap wandering characters
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1566: .vibe-weather has no z-index. Wander keyframes can position idle workers near the weather widget position. Add z-index: 2 to .vibe-weather in Office.css so it always renders above wandering characters.
